title: Neomammillaria goodridgei
intro: Neomammillaria goodridgei, commonly known as Goodridge's mammillaria, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, characterized by its small, globular stems and vibrant pink flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria goodridgei include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Neomammillaria goodridgei include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria goodridgei is classified within the family Cactaceae, which includes all types of cacti. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae, a group of cacti that includes species with a wide range of shapes and sizes. The genus Neomammillaria is a group of cacti characterized by their globular or cylindrical stems and small flowers. Neomammillaria goodridgei is also classified as a part of the tribe Cacteae, which includes some of the most diverse and widespread cacti in the Americas. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, distribution, and evolutionary history.
